action.reasoning: Observation: the only blocker is checkpoint loading mismatch, and stderr now reveals the exact saved xlsr_ft.pt head dimensions: pooled dim 2048 feeding a 256-d hidden layer, with BatchNorm over 256, not 512 or 2048. Analysis: we already have a strong checkpoint and no eval yet, so the highest-value move is to make model reconstruction exactly schema-aware and submit immediately. The checkpoint keys indicate baseline-style attentive stats pooling on XLS-R hidden size 1024 => pooled 2048, then Linear(2048->256), BN(256), ReLU, Dropout, Linear(256->1). We should rewrite model.py and predict.py compactly to infer this architecture from state_dict shapes, verify locally on both checkpoints, and submit xlsr_ft.pt in the same iteration. Decision: patch only loading/inference compatibility, smoke test, then submit eval immediately.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/xlsr_ft.pt --output tmp_pred.json && python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output tmp_pred.json
